_comment_ppm_offset: Software frequency calibration. Subtracted from every reported freq. ppm_offset > 0 means our reports were running too high (Pitaya TCXO slow). W3RGA's daily analysis is the authoritative source — set to whatever ppm value SM7IUN flags us at on the latest standings. compute_ppm_skew.py is useful for sanity-checking shifts but hits a 10 Hz tee-rounding floor (~1 ppm resolution). Hardware fix is the R25/R26 SMD mod + Bodnar 125 MHz GPSDO when convenient. CRITICAL LESSON (2026-05-07): allow 24+ hours of thermal soak after any band-count or load change before chasing a W3RGA skew flag. The +5.3 ppm flagged on Day 4 was thermal transient from the 04-30 8-band switch-on; correcting against it overshot to -4.8 (Day 5). After resetting to 0.0 the steady-state drift came in at +0.1 ppm on Day 6 — same as W3RGA himself. Don't react to one day's skew; wait for thermal equilibrium.
_comment_gates: Gate flags — defaults shown are 'ship-it' mode (permissive emit, trust cluster filtering). Each gate is a feature you can re-enable individually for stricter local behavior. See feedback_ship_it_plan.md for rationale.
_comment_timing_cost: ITILA timing-cost gate (ggmorse-inspired). Per decode window, libitila.so computes a confidence score on segmentation timing (lower = cleaner Morse). When gate_timing_cost is true, ITILA windows with cost > timing_cost_max are dropped before call extraction. B1_seg2 validation (40m CWT) suggested gate=30 = zero in-key recall loss. First 7h of production observation (2026-05-14) showed median spot cost 19 (vs B1's 0.26) and a flood of 3-char hallucinations (G5E 78x, M3E 25x, M5A 6x) at cost > 85. Threshold raised to 100 for the initial gate-on rollout — catches the obvious M5M-class flood, preserves the marginal 30-85 cost band until we see whether real calls live there in production. Ratchet down to 50, then 30 after observing the effect at 100.
_comment_recent_band: S-floor: peer-skimmer support cache (ported from N2WQ GoCluster). When peers spot a call on a band recently, our solo decode of the same call is anchored to a 1-sighting threshold. Peer threads start when peers list is non-empty regardless of gate flag — keeps the cache warm so we can flip the gate at runtime.
